data.body:
## Completion Verification ‚Äî PASS

All 12 acceptance criteria and all 5 success criteria verified against the repository. Every requirement is demonstrably met.

### Acceptance Criteria

**AC1 ‚Äî Single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema module**
MET. `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_snapshot_schema.py` exists as a TypedDict + JSON Schema SSOT covering all fields the differs read (`summary`, `description`, `status`, `priority`, `assignee`, `labels`, `issuetype`, `comment` (nested, bug 0ee6 seam), `issuelinks` (bug 3f04 seam), `parent`). It is referenced by `test_snapshot_contract.py`, `test_roundtrip.py`, and `tests/_jira_shape_contract.py`.

**AC2 ‚Äî Test validates fetcher's snapshot output against schema**
MET. `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_snapshot_contract.py` exists and drives `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` output through schema validation, and is designed to FAIL on the pre-fix conditions (issuelinks absent; comment under the wrong key).

**AC3 ‚Äî Test validates each differ's snapshot reads against schema**
MET. Covered by the round-trip contract test in `test_snapshot_contract.py`, which drives both `inbound_differ` and `outbound_differ` reads through the production path against the schema-validated snapshot.

**AC4 ‚Äî One round-trip contract test through the production path using real Jira fixtures**
MET. `test_snapshot_contract.py` + `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_roundtrip.py` exercise `FakeAcliClient ‚Üí fetcher.fetch_snapshot ‚Üí inbound_differ + outbound_differ ‚Üí applier` through the production path. Real captured fixtures exist at `tests/fixtures/jira/` (search.json, comment_map.json, issuelinks_map.json, parent_map.json, _meta.json).

**AC5 ‚Äî Outbound application collapsed to a single production path**
MET. `apply_outbound._apply_outbound_update` (apply_outbound.py:125‚Äì161) is now a thin delegator to `_mutation_to_batch_dict` + `update_one`, mirroring `_apply_outbound_delete`. The ~240-line parallel implementation is gone.

**AC6 ‚Äî Retired outbound applier path is deleted**
MET. The parallel apply logic is removed; old test files (`test_applier_outbound_update.py`, `test_applier_outbound_links.py`) are deleted. The leaf remains as a thin DISTINCT dispatch binding (required by the typed registry) but carries no parallel application logic.

**AC7 ‚Äî Outbound sub-op telemetry surfaced in the structured batch outcome**
MET. `applier.py:803‚Äì805` surfaces `links_applied`, `comments_applied`, `labels_applied` on the outcome dict from the `_subop` accumulator. `applier.py:865‚Äì869` surfaces all three on the `RECON: batch_outcome` line. `batch_dispatch.py:498/515/581/597` mints all as integer counters (post-dedup for links).

**AC8 ‚Äî `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly (warn-first)**
MET. `applier.py:814‚Äì836` implements the silent-no-op canary (warn-first by default; hard-fail behind `REBAR_RECONCILER_FAIL_SILENT_NOOP=1`). `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_silent_noop_canary.py` asserts both flag states, the dedup-no-false-positive case, and the link-drop reproduction.

**AC9 ‚Äî Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically**
MET. `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_roundtrip.py` (280 lines) covers inbound (Jira‚Üílocal) and outbound (local‚ÜíJira) for comments, links (both directions), and parent through the production code path with `pytestmark = pytest.mark.integration`.

**AC10 ‚Äî Every probe assertion encoding a bug as expected converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes**
MET. `scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:1545‚Äì1550` ‚Äî the previously inverted inbound-comments NOT-SYNCED guard is replaced with a positive assertion (`pass_test "Phase3.verify-inbound-comments (inbound comment synced ‚Äî bug 0ee6 closed)"`) that fails on regression. The only remaining `NOT-SYNCED` occurrence is an explanatory comment at line 1535 documenting the de-encoding.

**AC11 ‚Äî Documented anti-change-detector guardrail committed and referenced by new tests**
MET. `docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md` exists (ADR-style, status: Accepted). Referenced by `_snapshot_schema.py:22`, `test_snapshot_contract.py:18`, `test_roundtrip.py:22`, `test_outbound_update_combined.py:21`, and `tests/_jira_shape_contract.py:16`.

**AC12 ‚Äî All six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green**
MET. All six stories closed with signed completion-verifier PASS: fe57 (hermetic fixtures + fake), 8937 (schema + round-trip), 59ef (verified fake), 33d0 (applier collapse), 2359 (sub-op telemetry + canary), 822a (bidirectional round-trip + probe de-encoding). Last epic comment records 1043 passed.

### Success Criteria

- **SC1** Producer/consumer cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing ‚Äî MET (schema SSOT + joint round-trip contract).
- **SC2** Outbound sync exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path ‚Äî MET (single thin-delegator leaf ‚Üí update_one).
- **SC3** Silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green ‚Äî MET (warn-first canary in applier.py + test_silent_noop_canary.py).
- **SC4** Bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected ‚Äî MET (test_roundtrip.py + de-encoded probe).
- **SC5** New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic/type); suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added ‚Äî MET (ADR guardrail enforces this; all new tests assert structure/type/round-trip outcomes, not blob equality or mock-call-counts).

**Verdict: PASS**
